div.p: Subject: Yugoslav Candidacy for the Security Council

Mr. Hickerson met this noon with Sir Alexander Cadogan (UK)Permanent British Representative at the United Nations. and Ambassador Chauvel (France)Permanent French Representative at the United Nations. on this matter. He reiterated the US position which had previously been given to both delegations. He stressed the importance of reaching concerted view at the earliest possible moment. Sir Alexander and Mr. Chauvel both promised to review the matter and give us their views promptly.

Later in the day the French reported that Mr. Chauvel had talked with SchumanRobert Schuman, French Minister for Foreign Affairs and Head of the French Delegation to the General Assembly. who felt that the matter should be left alone for perhaps two more days and reviewed again at the end of that time. He felt that in the interim our delegations should neither encourage nor discourage the proposal.

The British later replied that Mr. BevinErnest Bevin, British Secretary of State for Foreign Affairs and Head of the British Delegation to the General Assembly. wanted to speak about the matter with the Secretary. They discussed the matter at Flushing in the later afternoon. The Secretary explained our position and stressed that he did not intend to “lobby” in any way for the proposal but did intend to vote for it in the event that we determined it would command general support in the Assembly. Mr. Bevin placed great emphasis on the matter of not campaigning for the proposal in any way and, based on that condition, agreed to our position.
